tips: Use grep -r for recursive search across files in a directory
Pipe commands together with | to build powerful one-liners
Use find with -exec to run commands on matching files
The history command shows your recent command history — use !n to replay
Use alias to create shortcuts for frequently used commands
Use watch to run a command repeatedly and see output changes
Use rsync for efficient file transfers and backups
The tree command visualizes directory structures
Use lsof to see which files and ports a process has open
Use htop for an interactive, human-friendly process manager
Use tmux to manage multiple terminal sessions in one window
The awk and sed commands are powerful for text processing
